questions.prompt: In his 1946 speech at Fulton, Missouri, Winston Churchill warned that an "iron curtain" had descended across Europe. To what development was he referring?
choices: The construction of fortified borders by the United Nations
The Soviet imposition of communist governments in Eastern Europe
The economic blockade of Western Europe by the Marshall Plan
The military occupation of Greece and Turkey by Nazi remnants
questions.correctText: The Soviet imposition of communist governments in Eastern Europe

questions.prompt: The Truman Doctrine of 1947 committed the United States to a policy of
choices: unconditional disarmament of former Axis powers
containing the spread of communism worldwide
establishing colonial protectorates in the Middle East
isolating the United States from European affairs
questions.correctText: containing the spread of communism worldwide

questions.prompt: The primary purpose of the Marshall Plan (1948) was to
choices: rearm West Germany against the Soviet Union
provide economic aid to rebuild war-torn Western Europe
establish a free-trade zone within the British Empire
fund the reconstruction of the Soviet economy
questions.correctText: provide economic aid to rebuild war-torn Western Europe

questions.prompt: The foreign policy of "containment" pursued by the United States during the Cold War was designed to
choices: roll back communism wherever it already existed
prevent the further expansion of Soviet influence
encourage decolonization throughout the developing world
limit the spread of nuclear technology to allied states
questions.correctText: prevent the further expansion of Soviet influence

questions.prompt: The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), formed in 1949, was created primarily as a
choices: collective security alliance against Soviet aggression
economic union to coordinate European recovery
trade bloc to compete with the British Commonwealth
judicial body to prosecute war criminals
questions.correctText: collective security alliance against Soviet aggression

questions.prompt: The Warsaw Pact, established in 1955, was the Soviet Union's response to
choices: the founding of the United Nations
the rearmament of West Germany and its admission to NATO
the Marshall Plan's economic aid programs
the Korean armistice at the 38th parallel
questions.correctText: the rearmament of West Germany and its admission to NATO

questions.prompt: The Berlin Airlift of 1948–1949 was the Western response to
choices: a Soviet ground blockade of West Berlin
the assassination of Konrad Adenauer
the construction of the Berlin Wall
the partition of Germany into four zones
questions.correctText: a Soviet ground blockade of West Berlin

questions.prompt: The division of Germany after World War II resulted in
choices: a single neutral state under United Nations supervision
the creation of the Federal Republic and the German Democratic Republic
the restoration of the prewar Weimar Republic
annexation by France and the Soviet Union
questions.correctText: the creation of the Federal Republic and the German Democratic Republic

questions.prompt: The Chinese Communist victory in 1949 was largely the result of
choices: direct Soviet military intervention against the Nationalists
peasant support and effective guerrilla tactics led by Mao Zedong
United Nations recognition of the Communist Party
the death of Chiang Kai-shek during the civil war
questions.correctText: peasant support and effective guerrilla tactics led by Mao Zedong

questions.prompt: Mao Zedong's Long March (1934–1935) is best understood as
choices: a successful invasion of Japanese-held Manchuria
a strategic retreat that preserved the Communist movement
a peasant uprising against Confucian landlords
a campaign to drive European missionaries from China
questions.correctText: a strategic retreat that preserved the Communist movement

questions.prompt: After 1949, Chiang Kai-shek and the Nationalists retreated to
choices: Hong Kong
Singapore
Taiwan
the Philippines
questions.correctText: Taiwan

questions.prompt: Mao Zedong's Great Leap Forward (1958–1962) is most closely associated with
choices: rapid democratization of village government
communal farming and a massive famine
a peace treaty with the Soviet Union
the establishment of special economic zones
questions.correctText: communal farming and a massive famine

questions.prompt: The Red Guards played a central role in which event?
choices: The Cultural Revolution
The Great Leap Forward
The Boxer Rebellion
The Tiananmen Square protests
questions.correctText: The Cultural Revolution

questions.prompt: One major goal of the Cultural Revolution (1966–1976) in China was to
choices: modernize Chinese industry through Western technology
purge perceived "bourgeois" elements and reassert Mao's authority
restore the imperial examination system
encourage rapprochement with Taiwan
questions.correctText: purge perceived "bourgeois" elements and reassert Mao's authority

questions.prompt: The Korean War (1950–1953) ended with
choices: the unification of Korea under a democratic government
an armistice that left Korea divided near the 38th parallel
the annexation of the peninsula by China
a United Nations protectorate over both Koreas
questions.correctText: an armistice that left Korea divided near the 38th parallel

questions.prompt: United Nations forces in the Korean War were dispatched primarily because
choices: North Korea had invaded South Korea across the 38th parallel
Japan had refused to accept the postwar peace settlement
China had threatened nuclear retaliation against Taiwan
the Soviet Union had declared war on the United States
questions.correctText: North Korea had invaded South Korea across the 38th parallel

questions.prompt: The Cuban Missile Crisis of 1962 brought the world closest to
choices: a Latin American economic union
nuclear war between the United States and the Soviet Union
a Soviet invasion of Western Europe
Chinese intervention in the Caribbean
questions.correctText: nuclear war between the United States and the Soviet Union

questions.prompt: The Cuban Missile Crisis was resolved when
choices: the United States invaded Cuba and overthrew Castro
Khrushchev agreed to withdraw Soviet missiles in exchange for U.S. concessions
the United Nations occupied Cuba with peacekeeping troops
China brokered a treaty between Washington and Moscow
questions.correctText: Khrushchev agreed to withdraw Soviet missiles in exchange for U.S. concessions

questions.prompt: The Geneva Accords of 1954 temporarily divided Vietnam at the
choices: 17th parallel
38th parallel
49th parallel
Mekong Delta
questions.correctText: 17th parallel

questions.prompt: The French Indochina War ended in 1954 with the French defeat at
choices: Hue
Dien Bien Phu
Saigon
Hanoi
questions.correctText: Dien Bien Phu

questions.prompt: Ngo Dinh Diem, supported by the United States, served as the leader of
choices: North Vietnam
South Vietnam
Cambodia under the Khmer Rouge
Laos during the Cold War
questions.correctText: South Vietnam

questions.prompt: The Tet Offensive of 1968 was significant primarily because it
choices: ended the Vietnam War with a North Vietnamese surrender
shifted American public opinion against continued involvement in Vietnam
marked the first use of nuclear weapons by the United States
established a permanent ceasefire along the demilitarized zone
questions.correctText: shifted American public opinion against continued involvement in Vietnam

questions.prompt: The Paris Peace Accords of 1973 led directly to
choices: the withdrawal of remaining United States combat forces from Vietnam
the reunification of Vietnam under Diem's government
the partition of Vietnam into three separate states
American military intervention in Cambodia
questions.correctText: the withdrawal of remaining United States combat forces from Vietnam

questions.prompt: The fall of Saigon in 1975 resulted in
choices: the reunification of Vietnam under communist rule
the establishment of a United Nations protectorate
a renewed French colonial presence in Indochina
the partition of Vietnam into four zones
questions.correctText: the reunification of Vietnam under communist rule

questions.prompt: Ghana, which gained independence in 1957 under Kwame Nkrumah, was significant because it was
choices: the first sub-Saharan African colony to win independence from European rule
the last African nation to abolish apartheid
the founding state of the Arab League
the only African colony liberated by armed revolution
questions.correctText: the first sub-Saharan African colony to win independence from European rule

questions.prompt: The Mau Mau uprising of the 1950s was an anticolonial revolt against
choices: French rule in Algeria
British rule in Kenya
Belgian rule in the Congo
Portuguese rule in Mozambique
questions.correctText: British rule in Kenya

questions.prompt: The Algerian War of Independence (1954–1962) was fought primarily against
choices: British colonial authorities
French colonial authorities
Italian colonial authorities
Spanish colonial authorities
questions.correctText: French colonial authorities

questions.prompt: Apartheid in South Africa was a system of
choices: voluntary cultural exchange among ethnic groups
rigid racial segregation enforced by law
shared political authority across racial lines
land redistribution to indigenous peoples
questions.correctText: rigid racial segregation enforced by law

questions.prompt: Nelson Mandela was imprisoned in 1962 because of his
choices: leadership of the African National Congress and opposition to apartheid
alliance with the Soviet Union during the Cold War
support for the policies of Hendrik Verwoerd
role in the partition of South Africa
questions.correctText: leadership of the African National Congress and opposition to apartheid

questions.prompt: The Sharpeville Massacre of 1960 demonstrated to the world the
choices: determination of the United Nations to end apartheid through force
violence used by the South African state against peaceful protesters
Soviet Union's policy of nonintervention in Africa
willingness of the British Commonwealth to defend white minority rule
questions.correctText: violence used by the South African state against peaceful protesters

questions.prompt: Apartheid in South Africa was formally dismantled in
Nelson Mandela's election as president in 1994 marked
choices: the founding of the Republic of South Africa
the first multiracial democratic election in South Africa
the secession of the Cape Province from South Africa
the return of South Africa to British dominion status
questions.correctText: the first multiracial democratic election in South Africa

questions.prompt: The 1947 partition of British India created two independent states:
choices: India and Bangladesh
India and Pakistan
Pakistan and Sri Lanka
India and Burma
questions.correctText: India and Pakistan

questions.prompt: The partition of India in 1947 led to
choices: the peaceful exchange of populations across the new border
massive violence and refugee flows between Hindus and Muslims
the immediate creation of Bangladesh
the restoration of Mughal rule in Delhi
questions.correctText: massive violence and refugee flows between Hindus and Muslims

questions.prompt: Mohandas Gandhi was assassinated in 1948 by
choices: a British colonial soldier
a Hindu nationalist opposed to his conciliatory stance toward Muslims
a member of the Muslim League
a soldier in the Indian National Army
questions.correctText: a Hindu nationalist opposed to his conciliatory stance toward Muslims

questions.prompt: Jawaharlal Nehru, India's first prime minister, pursued a foreign policy of
choices: nonalignment between the Cold War blocs
active membership in the Warsaw Pact
formal alliance with the United States
permanent neutrality within the British Commonwealth
questions.correctText: nonalignment between the Cold War blocs

questions.prompt: Bangladesh emerged as an independent nation in 1971 after seceding from
choices: India
Pakistan
Burma
Sri Lanka
questions.correctText: Pakistan

questions.prompt: The modern state of Israel was founded in
The Six-Day War of 1967 resulted in Israel's occupation of
choices: the Sinai Peninsula, West Bank, Gaza Strip, and Golan Heights
only the Sinai Peninsula
the Suez Canal Zone
the entire Arabian Peninsula
questions.correctText: the Sinai Peninsula, West Bank, Gaza Strip, and Golan Heights

questions.prompt: The Yom Kippur War of 1973 began when
choices: Israel invaded Lebanon to combat the PLO
Egypt and Syria launched a coordinated surprise attack on Israel
Iraq invaded Kuwait under Saddam Hussein
Iran attempted to seize the Strait of Hormuz
questions.correctText: Egypt and Syria launched a coordinated surprise attack on Israel

questions.prompt: The Camp David Accords of 1978 produced a peace agreement between
choices: Israel and Jordan
Israel and Egypt
Israel and Syria
Israel and the Palestine Liberation Organization
questions.correctText: Israel and Egypt

questions.prompt: The Intifadas of the late 1980s and early 2000s were primarily
choices: Israeli settler movements in the occupied territories
Palestinian uprisings against Israeli rule in the West Bank and Gaza
Arab League invasions of Israel
United Nations peacekeeping missions
questions.correctText: Palestinian uprisings against Israeli rule in the West Bank and Gaza

questions.prompt: The Iranian Revolution of 1979 resulted in
choices: the restoration of the Pahlavi monarchy
the establishment of an Islamic Republic under Ayatollah Khomeini
the partition of Iran between Iraq and the Soviet Union
Iran's accession to NATO
questions.correctText: the establishment of an Islamic Republic under Ayatollah Khomeini

questions.prompt: The Iran hostage crisis (1979–1981) involved
choices: the seizure of the United States embassy in Tehran by Iranian revolutionaries
the kidnapping of OPEC officials in Vienna
the capture of Soviet diplomats in Kabul
the detention of British oil workers in Abadan
questions.correctText: the seizure of the United States embassy in Tehran by Iranian revolutionaries

questions.prompt: Fidel Castro came to power in Cuba in 1959 after
choices: a free and fair democratic election
overthrowing the Batista regime through guerrilla warfare
negotiating independence from the United States
succeeding his father as president for life
questions.correctText: overthrowing the Batista regime through guerrilla warfare

questions.prompt: In 1973, the democratically elected president of Chile, Salvador Allende, was overthrown by a military coup led by
choices: Augusto Pinochet
Juan Perón
Fulgencio Batista
Anastasio Somoza
questions.correctText: Augusto Pinochet

questions.prompt: United States Cold War interventions in Latin America were often motivated by a desire to
choices: prevent the spread of leftist or communist governments in the hemisphere
establish formal colonial control over Central American republics
promote the agenda of the Non-Aligned Movement
support land reform proposed by indigenous parties
questions.correctText: prevent the spread of leftist or communist governments in the hemisphere

questions.prompt: The Strategic Arms Limitation Talks (SALT) of the early 1970s were part of a broader policy known as
choices: détente
containment
rollback
massive retaliation
questions.correctText: détente

questions.prompt: The Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces (INF) Treaty of 1987 was significant because it
choices: eliminated an entire class of nuclear missiles held by the superpowers
ended the Vietnam War on terms favorable to North Vietnam
established the European Union
expanded NATO into Eastern Europe
questions.correctText: eliminated an entire class of nuclear missiles held by the superpowers

questions.prompt: The START treaties, signed by the United States and the Soviet Union (later Russia), focused on
choices: the reduction of strategic nuclear arsenals
the demilitarization of outer space
trade liberalization in Eastern Europe
the banning of chemical weapons
questions.correctText: the reduction of strategic nuclear arsenals

questions.prompt: Mikhail Gorbachev's policy of glasnost called for
choices: restructuring of the Soviet economy
openness in political discussion and the press
expanded military spending against NATO
the recentralization of Communist Party control
questions.correctText: openness in political discussion and the press

questions.prompt: Gorbachev's policy of perestroika sought to
choices: restructure the Soviet economy by introducing limited market reforms
tighten censorship of Soviet media
expand the Warsaw Pact into the Middle East
establish a hereditary Soviet presidency
questions.correctText: restructure the Soviet economy by introducing limited market reforms

questions.prompt: The fall of the Berlin Wall on November 9, 1989 symbolized
choices: the beginning of the Cold War
the collapse of communist control over Eastern Europe
the founding of the European Union
the end of World War II reparations
questions.correctText: the collapse of communist control over Eastern Europe

questions.prompt: Between 1989 and 1991, Eastern European communist regimes fell in part because
choices: Gorbachev refused to use Soviet military force to maintain them
Western European armies invaded the Warsaw Pact states
China provided economic aid to anticommunist movements
the United Nations imposed sanctions on Moscow
questions.correctText: Gorbachev refused to use Soviet military force to maintain them

questions.prompt: The Soviet Union officially dissolved in
The end of the Cold War is most directly associated with
choices: the signing of the Treaty of Versailles
the dissolution of the Soviet Union and the collapse of Eastern bloc communism
the founding of the United Nations
the partition of Korea
questions.correctText: the dissolution of the Soviet Union and the collapse of Eastern bloc communism

questions.prompt: The Khmer Rouge regime led by Pol Pot ruled which country from 1975 to 1979?
choices: Vietnam
Cambodia
Laos
Burma
questions.correctText: Cambodia

questions.prompt: The "killing fields" of Cambodia refer to
choices: mass executions and deaths under the Khmer Rouge regime
battlefields of the Vietnam War in the Mekong Delta
agricultural reforms in postcolonial Africa
Buddhist pilgrimage sites destroyed by colonial powers
questions.correctText: mass executions and deaths under the Khmer Rouge regime

questions.prompt: The Rwandan genocide of 1994 was characterized by
choices: mass killings of Tutsi by Hutu extremists
an invasion of Rwanda by Belgian colonial forces
a civil war between Christian and Muslim communities
United Nations military intervention that halted the violence
questions.correctText: mass killings of Tutsi by Hutu extremists

questions.prompt: The 1995 massacre at Srebrenica was committed against
choices: Bosnian Muslim men and boys by Bosnian Serb forces
Croat civilians by Yugoslav army units
Albanian Kosovars by NATO airstrikes
Macedonian peacekeepers by Greek nationalists
questions.correctText: Bosnian Muslim men and boys by Bosnian Serb forces

questions.prompt: The conflict in Darfur that escalated after 2003 took place in
choices: Sudan
Somalia
Chad
Eritrea
questions.correctText: Sudan

questions.prompt: Beginning in 1978, Deng Xiaoping's reforms moved China toward
choices: greater state control of agriculture and industry
a market-oriented economy while maintaining Communist Party rule
Western-style multiparty democracy
complete isolation from global trade
questions.correctText: a market-oriented economy while maintaining Communist Party rule

questions.prompt: The Tiananmen Square protests of 1989 ended when
choices: Chinese leaders agreed to political reform
the Chinese government used military force to suppress demonstrators
the United Nations imposed sanctions on China
Deng Xiaoping resigned from power
questions.correctText: the Chinese government used military force to suppress demonstrators

questions.prompt: China's accession to the World Trade Organization in 2001 marked
choices: the formal end of the Chinese Communist Party
China's deeper integration into the global economy
Taiwan's reunification with the mainland
the privatization of all Chinese state enterprises
questions.correctText: China's deeper integration into the global economy

questions.prompt: The World Trade Organization (WTO), established in 1995, was created primarily to
choices: promote and regulate global trade among member states
manage United Nations peacekeeping missions
oversee European monetary union
coordinate international environmental policy
questions.correctText: promote and regulate global trade among member states

questions.prompt: The North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), which took effect in 1994, removed many trade barriers among
choices: the United States, Canada, and Mexico
the United States, Brazil, and Argentina
the United States, Britain, and France
the United States, Japan, and South Korea
questions.correctText: the United States, Canada, and Mexico

questions.prompt: The euro was introduced as a common European currency in
Multinational corporations are best characterized as
choices: businesses that operate production and sales across multiple countries
agencies of the United Nations focused on trade
religious organizations promoting global missionary work
associations of national governments coordinating tariff policy
questions.correctText: businesses that operate production and sales across multiple countries

questions.prompt: "Outsourcing" in the late twentieth and early twenty-first centuries most often refers to
choices: the relocation of jobs and production to lower-wage countries
the nationalization of foreign-owned industries
the migration of skilled workers from rural to urban areas
the closure of overseas embassies
questions.correctText: the relocation of jobs and production to lower-wage countries

questions.prompt: The rapid growth of the Internet since the 1990s has most directly contributed to
choices: the global spread of information and instant communication
the decline of literacy in industrialized nations
the end of multinational corporate activity
the strengthening of state monopolies on news media
questions.correctText: the global spread of information and instant communication

questions.prompt: The Kyoto Protocol (1997) and the Paris Agreement (2015) were international efforts to address
choices: nuclear proliferation
global climate change and greenhouse gas emissions
human trafficking
international terrorism
questions.correctText: global climate change and greenhouse gas emissions

questions.prompt: The September 11, 2001 attacks on the United States were carried out by
choices: agents of the Iraqi government
members of the al-Qaeda terrorist network led by Osama bin Laden
Iranian Revolutionary Guard commandos
Chechen separatists based in Moscow
questions.correctText: members of the al-Qaeda terrorist network led by Osama bin Laden

questions.prompt: The U.S.-led invasion of Afghanistan in 2001 was undertaken primarily to
choices: overthrow the Taliban regime that had sheltered al-Qaeda
establish a permanent American colony in Central Asia
secure Afghanistan's oil reserves
restore the Afghan monarchy
questions.correctText: overthrow the Taliban regime that had sheltered al-Qaeda

questions.prompt: The U.S.-led invasion of Iraq in 2003 was officially justified by claims that the Iraqi regime
choices: was harboring stockpiles of weapons of mass destruction
had attacked the United States on September 11
had invaded Saudi Arabia
had withdrawn from the United Nations
questions.correctText: was harboring stockpiles of weapons of mass destruction

questions.prompt: The series of popular uprisings across the Arab world that began in late 2010 is collectively known as the
choices: Arab Spring
Arab Awakening of 1979
First Intifada
Suez Crisis
questions.correctText: Arab Spring

questions.prompt: The Arab Spring uprisings led to the overthrow of long-standing rulers in
choices: Tunisia, Egypt, Libya, and Yemen
Saudi Arabia, Jordan, and Morocco
Iran, Iraq, and Lebanon
Israel, Turkey, and Cyprus
questions.correctText: Tunisia, Egypt, Libya, and Yemen

questions.prompt: The Syrian Civil War that began in 2011 grew out of
choices: protests against Bashar al-Assad's government during the Arab Spring
an invasion of Syria by Saudi Arabia
a coup led by Syrian Kurdish forces
Israel's annexation of the Golan Heights
questions.correctText: protests against Bashar al-Assad's government during the Arab Spring

questions.prompt: The COVID-19 pandemic that emerged in 2020 most directly demonstrated
choices: the speed at which diseases can spread in a globalized world
the collapse of the World Trade Organization
the end of international air travel forever
the success of the Kyoto Protocol
questions.correctText: the speed at which diseases can spread in a globalized world

questions.prompt: The Berlin Wall was constructed in 1961 mainly to
choices: prevent East Germans from fleeing to West Berlin
defend East Berlin from a feared NATO invasion
physically join the two halves of Berlin
mark the boundary of the Marshall Plan
questions.correctText: prevent East Germans from fleeing to West Berlin

questions.prompt: Which conflict is best described as a Cold War "proxy war"?
choices: The Vietnam War
World War I
The Spanish-American War
The Napoleonic Wars
questions.correctText: The Vietnam War

questions.prompt: One major reason for decolonization in Africa and Asia after World War II was
choices: the weakening of European powers and the rise of nationalist movements
the imposition of the gold standard by the United States
the founding of the European Coal and Steel Community
the spread of the bubonic plague across Europe
questions.correctText: the weakening of European powers and the rise of nationalist movements

questions.prompt: Which leader is best associated with the African National Congress and the struggle to end apartheid?
choices: Nelson Mandela
Jomo Kenyatta
Kwame Nkrumah
Patrice Lumumba
questions.correctText: Nelson Mandela

questions.prompt: The Korean War is often described as the first "hot" war of the Cold War because it
choices: involved direct combat between forces aligned with rival Cold War blocs
led to the use of atomic weapons in Asia
produced the only United Nations General Assembly war declaration
ended the alliance between the Soviet Union and China
questions.correctText: involved direct combat between forces aligned with rival Cold War blocs

questions.prompt: Which event is most closely associated with the year 1989?
choices: The fall of the Berlin Wall and the Tiananmen Square protests
The founding of NATO and the Warsaw Pact
The signing of the Camp David Accords
The partition of India and the founding of Israel
questions.correctText: The fall of the Berlin Wall and the Tiananmen Square protests

questions.prompt: Mahatma Gandhi's strategy of "satyagraha" emphasized
choices: nonviolent resistance to British rule
armed insurrection against colonial authorities
religious unification of Hindus and Sikhs
industrialization of rural India
questions.correctText: nonviolent resistance to British rule

questions.prompt: The Cold War rivalry between the United States and the Soviet Union was rooted in
choices: ideological differences between capitalist democracy and communism
competition over colonies in sub-Saharan Africa
disagreement over the size of the British Empire
disputes over the boundaries of Latin America
questions.correctText: ideological differences between capitalist democracy and communism

questions.prompt: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948) was adopted by the
choices: League of Nations
United Nations General Assembly
International Court of Justice
World Trade Organization
questions.correctText: United Nations General Assembly

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the impact of the Cold War on the developing world?
choices: Superpower rivalry often turned local conflicts into proxy wars
Developing nations were largely shielded from Cold War tensions
The United Nations succeeded in preventing all Cold War conflicts
Developing countries received no economic aid from either superpower
questions.correctText: Superpower rivalry often turned local conflicts into proxy wars

questions.prompt: Which factor most directly contributed to economic globalization in the late 20th century?
choices: Advances in transportation, communication, and trade liberalization
The expansion of the Warsaw Pact
The collapse of multinational corporations
The reintroduction of mercantilist tariffs
questions.correctText: Advances in transportation, communication, and trade liberalization

questions.prompt: The Truman Doctrine was first invoked to support governments in
choices: Greece and Turkey
Cuba and Mexico
Korea and Japan
Egypt and Iran
questions.correctText: Greece and Turkey

questions.prompt: The People's Republic of China was officially proclaimed in
Special Economic Zones established under Deng Xiaoping were designed to
choices: attract foreign investment and promote export-led growth
isolate China from Western influences
guarantee permanent employment for state workers
enforce strict price controls
questions.correctText: attract foreign investment and promote export-led growth

questions.prompt: The Sharpeville Massacre and the imprisonment of Nelson Mandela were both responses by the South African government to
choices: organized opposition to apartheid
Cuban military intervention in southern Africa
British attempts to recolonize South Africa
Soviet support for the National Party
questions.correctText: organized opposition to apartheid

questions.prompt: The Iranian Revolution most directly weakened
choices: a long-time United States ally in the Persian Gulf
the Soviet alliance system in Eastern Europe
the British Commonwealth
Chinese influence in Southeast Asia
questions.correctText: a long-time United States ally in the Persian Gulf

questions.prompt: The Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) emerged in the 1960s as
choices: the principal political voice of Palestinian nationalism
a moderate Israeli political party
an Egyptian-led peacekeeping force
a religious organization advocating Sunni-Shia reconciliation
questions.correctText: the principal political voice of Palestinian nationalism

questions.prompt: Which best describes the role of the United Nations during the Cold War?
choices: It often served as a forum for superpower diplomacy but was limited by vetoes in the Security Council
It commanded armies that prevented all major conflicts
It replaced the sovereignty of individual states
It was abolished by the Warsaw Pact
questions.correctText: It often served as a forum for superpower diplomacy but was limited by vetoes in the Security Council

questions.prompt: The Bay of Pigs invasion of 1961 was a failed attempt to
choices: overthrow Fidel Castro using Cuban exiles backed by the United States
depose Salvador Allende in Chile
remove Anastasio Somoza in Nicaragua
topple Saddam Hussein in Iraq
questions.correctText: overthrow Fidel Castro using Cuban exiles backed by the United States

questions.prompt: Which development best illustrates the global "War on Terror" launched after 2001?
choices: United States military operations in Afghanistan and Iraq
The signing of the Camp David Accords
The expansion of the Warsaw Pact
The fall of the Berlin Wall
questions.correctText: United States military operations in Afghanistan and Iraq

questions.prompt: A common feature of the genocides in Cambodia, Rwanda, Bosnia, and Darfur was
choices: mass killings of civilians targeted on the basis of identity
successful prevention by United Nations peacekeepers
the absence of any international response
destruction limited to military combatants
questions.correctText: mass killings of civilians targeted on the basis of identity

questions.prompt: Which statement best summarizes a major outcome of the late 20th-century world?
choices: The end of the Cold War, accelerated globalization, and new transnational challenges
The return of European colonial empires across Africa and Asia
The dissolution of the United Nations and the World Bank
A permanent halt to nuclear proliferation
questions.correctText: The end of the Cold War, accelerated globalization, and new transnational challenges
